data.title: Code-level review tier immutability enforcement
data.description:
## Context
When a DSO sprint orchestrator commits a complex multi-file change, review-complexity-classifier.sh scores the diff and selects a review tier — light (haiku), standard (sonnet), or deep (3x sonnet + opus synthesis). The deeper tiers catch architectural issues and cross-cutting regressions that lighter tiers miss. However, the TIER IMMUTABILITY rule that prevents an agent from substituting a lighter reviewer is enforced only by LLM guidance in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md. An agent under time pressure can re-run the classifier, dispatch a lighter agent, or ignore the tier entirely — and no script will catch it. The result: security-critical or high-blast-radius changes silently receive inadequate review, and the team has no way to detect the downgrade after the fact.

## Success Criteria
1. record-review.sh reads the last entry from ARTIFACTS_DIR/classifier-telemetry.jsonl, extracts selected_tier, and compares against review_tier in reviewer-findings.json — rejecting if findings tier ranks below classified tier
2. reviewer-findings.json includes a review_tier field (light|standard|deep) written via write-reviewer-findings.sh --review-tier, and validate-review-output.sh accepts the extended schema
3. Tier downgrade detected: record-review.sh exits non-zero, prints to stderr: classified tier, review tier, and instructions to re-run /dso:review
4. Missing/unreadable telemetry: fail-open — allow review, emit warning to stderr, record tier_verified=false in review-status
5. Tier enforcement resets when diff changes — no lock files, telemetry read fresh each invocation, existing diff_hash handles staleness
6. End-to-end downgrade test: classify a diff as standard, write findings with review_tier=light, invoke record-review.sh, confirm non-zero exit with error identifying the mismatch
7. End-to-end fail-open test: remove classifier-telemetry.jsonl, invoke record-review.sh with valid findings, confirm exit 0, warning on stderr, and tier_verified=false in review-status

## Scenario Analysis
- Missing review_tier field (critical): agent omits --review-tier — treated as fail-open + warning
- Tier string casing (medium): write-reviewer-findings.sh validates enum light|standard|deep (case-sensitive)
- Cross-worktree contamination (high): get_artifacts_dir() hashes REPO_ROOT per-worktree — verify in implementation
- reviewer-hash integrity (critical): hash computed after review_tier written — new files consistent; old files fail hash check (re-run review)
- CI lacks telemetry (high): fail-open per SC4 — acceptable for CI
- Fail-closed not configurable (medium): out of scope; future config key if requested

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Add --review-tier flag to write-reviewer-findings.sh with enum validation (light|standard|deep). Update validate-review-output.sh schema. In record-review.sh, read last line of ARTIFACTS_DIR/classifier-telemetry.jsonl, parse selected_tier, compare via rank function (light=1, standard=2, deep=3). Reject if findings rank < telemetry rank. On missing telemetry or missing review_tier, fail-open with warning and tier_verified=false.
